actions: EVACUATE UPWARD: Move immediately to the highest floor or roof. Do not climb into a closed attic.
STAY OUT OF WATER: Just 6 inches (15cm) of moving water can knock you down. 1 foot sweeps a car.
DISCONNECT POWER: Turn off main power and gas if water hasn't reached the panel yet. DO NOT touch wet electrical panels.
GRAB ESSENTIALS: Take your 'Go Bag' (ID, medications, phone, charger, sealed water).
UNBUCKLE immediately.
ROLL DOWN WINDOWS before the water reaches them. If power fails, use a window breaker or kick the side windows at the corner.
DO NOT OPEN THE DOOR. Water pressure will rush in and sink the car faster.
CLIMB OUT through the window and get to the roof of the car or higher ground.

keywords: น้ำท่วมฉับพลัน
น้ำป่าไหลหลาก
actions: ABANDON VEHICLES/BELONGINGS: Your life is more important.
RUN FOR HIGH GROUND: Do not wait. Flash floods can arrive in seconds.
AVOID CANYONS/DITCHES: Flash floods funnel through low points instantly.
STAY CALM: Keep the area completely still to slow venom spread.
REMOVE CONSTRICTIONS: Take off rings, watches, or tight clothing near the bite before swelling starts.
DO NOT SUCK VENOM or cut the wound. Do not apply ice.
SEEK MEDICAL HELP: Get to a hospital or medical hub immediately.
DO NOT TOUCH THE PERSON if they are still attached to the current.
TURN OFF POWER at the main breaker if possible.
USE NON-CONDUCTIVE OBJECT (like dry wood or plastic) to push the person away from the source.
STAY CLEAR OF PUDDLES near downed power lines—water conducts electricity.
APPLY DIRECT PRESSURE: Use a clean cloth, shirt, or hands and press hard on the wound.
ELEVATE THE WOUND above the heart if possible.
CLEAN IT LATER: Stopping major blood loss takes priority over dirt during a disaster.
IF BLEEDING DOES NOT STOP, apply a tourniquet 2 inches above the wound (only for limbs in extreme cases).
DO NOT DRINK FLOODWATER. It is contaminated with sewage and chemicals.
BOIL WATER: A rolling boil for 1 minute (3 minutes at high altitude) kills most pathogens.
BLEACH METHOD: If boiling is impossible, add 8 drops of regular unscented household bleach per gallon (4 liters) of clear water. Wait 30 mins.
water_purification.searchQuery: wikiHow purify water in an emergency

actions: CHECK RESPONSIVENESS & Get them to solid ground immediately.
TILT HEAD BACK, lift chin to open airway. Check for breathing (10 seconds max).
START CPR: If not breathing, push hard and fast in the center of the chest (100-120 pushes a minute).
CONTINUE until they breathe or medical help arrives.

keywords: อุณหภูมิตก
actions: REMOVE WET CLOTHING immediately. Getting out of wet clothes stops rapid heat loss.
WARM THE CENTER of the body first (chest, neck, head, groin) using dry blankets or skin-to-skin contact.
DO NOT vigorously rub the skin or put them in a hot bath (can cause heart failure).
PROVIDE WARM, SWEET NON-ALCOHOLIC DRINKS if the person is fully conscious.

keywords: ขยับไม่ได้
เจ็บกระดูก
actions: STOP ANY BLEEDING first by applying pressure around (not on) the broken bone.
IMMOBILIZE THE AREA: Do not try to realign the bone. Use rolled up newspapers, wood, or folded cardboard as a splint.
APPLY ICE (wrapped in cloth) to reduce swelling.
TREAT FOR SHOCK: Lay the person flat, elevate legs if breathing is OK, and keep them warm.

keywords: ติดอยู่ใต้
ซากปรักหักพัง
หลังคาถล่ม
actions: COVER YOUR MOUTH with a shirt or cloth to prevent dust inhalation.
DO NOT SHOUT continuously—you will exhaust yourself and inhale dust. Tap on a pipe or wall rhythmically (3 quick, 3 slow, 3 quick).
IF YOU ARE FREE, do not enter an unstable building to save others. Call out to them and mark the location.
TURN OFF GAS/ELECTRICITY at the mains if safe to reach.
STAY AWAKE AND ALERT: Many deaths occur when people are sleeping. Listen for unusual sounds like trees cracking.
MOVE OUT OF THE PATH: If you hear a mudslide coming, run sideways (perpendicular) to the slide's path. Do not run straight downhill.
IF ESCAPE IS IMPOSSIBLE, curl into a tight ball and protect your head.
AVOID RIVER VALLEYS and low-lying areas during heavy continuous rain.

keywords: ห้องใต้หลังคา
actions: DO NOT ENTER A CLOSED ATTIC. You may become trapped by rising water. Always stay on the exterior roof.
MAKE YOURSELF VISIBLE: Hang a brightly colored sheet or clothing. Use a mirror or glass to reflect sunlight at helicopters.
CONSERVE PHONE BATTERY: Texting uses less battery than calling. Turn down screen brightness.
STAY PUT: Do not attempt to swim through floodwaters unless the structure is collapsing.
stranded_roof.searchQuery: wikiHow signal for help on a roof

keywords: พิษสุนัขบ้า
actions: WASH THE WOUND IMMEDIATELY with soap and clean water for at least 15 minutes. This severely reduces rabies risk.
APPLY ANTIBIOTIC OINTMENT and cover with a sterile bandage.
SEEK MEDICAL HELP: Flood waters push animals into human areas. Rabies and Tetanus vaccines may be required ASAP.
DO NOT attempt to capture the animal.

keywords: น้ำมันรั่ว
actions: EVACUATE UPWIND AND UPHILL: Move away from the smell or visible spill immediately.
WASH EXPOSED SKIN/EYES with copious amounts of clean water for 15 minutes.
DO NOT USE FLAMES: If you smell gas, do not light matches, candles, or turn on electrical switches (creates sparks).
REMOVE CONTAMINATED CLOTHING and seal it in a plastic bag.

keywords: ไม่มีไฟฟ้า
actions: USE FLASHLIGHTS, NOT CANDLES, to prevent fire hazards.
KEEP FRIDGE CLOSED: An unopened refrigerator will keep food cold for about 4 hours. A full freezer keeps temperature for 48 hours.
UNPLUG ELECTRONICS to protect them from power surges when electricity is restored.
CONSERVE PHONE BATTERY: Turn on power-saving mode and only use for emergencies.

keywords: หาลูกไม่เจอ
actions: CHECK WATER FIRST: If near floodwater, check the water immediately. Seconds count.
STAY IN ONE PLACE: Have one adult stay exactly where the child was last seen.
ALERT AUTHORITIES & HUBS: Check the local Community Hubs (Mosques/Schools) listed in this app.
WRITE DOWN details: Age, height, weight, exact clothing worn, and a recent picture.
lost_child.searchQuery: wikiHow to find a lost child

actions: HYDRATE CONSTANTLY: Drink clean, purified water (or oral rehydration solutions) to replace lost fluids from vomiting/diarrhea.
WASH HANDS rigorously with soap and clean water to prevent spreading waterborne diseases (Cholera, Typhoid).
REDUCE FEVER using Paracetamol/Acetaminophen. Do not use Aspirin on children.
ISOLATE the sick person from healthy family members if possible.
fever_infection.searchQuery: wikiHow to treat fever and diarrhea

keywords: ไม่มีสัญญาณ
ขอความช่วยเหลือ
actions: USE SMS TEXT MESSAGES: Texts often go through when voice calls cannot because they require far less bandwidth.
CHANGE YOUR VOICEMAIL: If you have 1 bar of signal, quickly change your voicemail greeting to state your exact location and status.
WHISTLE OR MIRROR: Use a whistle (3 short blasts is universal SOS) or mirror reflections to attract helicopters/boats.
TRUST THIS APP: The 'Find Safe Hubs' and this safety dictionary work 100% offline.

keywords: น้ำร้อนลวก
actions: COOL THE WOUND immediately with cool (not ice cold) running water for 10-20 minutes.
DO NOT POP BLISTERS or apply ice, butter, or ointments to severe burns.
COVER THE BURN lightly with a sterile, non-fluffy dressing or plastic wrap to prevent infection.
REMOVE JEWELRY or tight items near the burn before it begins to swell.
DO NOT DRIVE INTO WATER. Turn around, don't drown. The road underneath may be washed away.
FIND HIGH, SOLID GROUND. Do not attempt to cross moving water on foot if it's above your ankles.
SEEK SHELTER IN PLACE: If safe to do so, return to a sturdy building and move to the upper floors.
USE THE HUB MAP: Check the local offline Hub Map in this app to find the nearest Mosque/School.
evacuation_route_blocked.searchQuery: wikiHow to survive being stranded in a car

actions: IF RED RISK: ABANDON GOODS. Evacuate immediately. Your life is worth more than inventory.
IF YELLOW/ORANGE RISK: Move valuable/electronic goods to the highest possible shelves or second floor.
DO NOT LOAD A CAR if the water is already rising fast. Cars become death traps in fast-moving water.
SANDBAG ENTRANCES: Use sandbags at doors/drains to delay water entry if time permits.
CUT MAIN POWER to the shop to prevent electrical fires if water enters.
shop_owner_flood.searchQuery: wikiHow protect a store from flooding

keywords: โรคประจำตัว
actions: STOCK UP EARLY: Buy a 14-30 day supply of essential medications (insulin, heart meds) at the first Green/Yellow warning.
PROTECT INSULIN: If power fails, keep insulin in a sealed waterproof container. Do not let it freeze; keep it cool.
WATERPROOF COPIES: Place hard copies of all prescriptions and doctor contacts in a waterproof ziplock bag.
PACK A MEDICAL GO-BAG: Ensure all daily medical apparatuses (blood sugar monitors, test strips, batteries) are packed first.
chronic_illness_prep.searchQuery: wikiHow prepare medication for an emergency

actions: Assessing situation from keywords...
STAY CALM and breathe.
Move to the safest, highest area accessible.
Wait for AI engine to download, or listen to local radio/Hubs for updates.
